# GoOMN: Cross-Platform Markdown Note Editor

GoOMN is a lightweight, cross-platform note-taking application specifically designed to replace Open Markdown Notes. It features an isolated Go backend, an ultra-slim Android APK capability via `gomobile`, and an HTML/Tailwind frontend.

## 📁 File Structure (Generated by `setup_project.py`)

Once initialized via the Python script, your directory will match this architecture:

```text
GoOMN/
├── setup_project.py     # Core project generator and updater (for future turns)
├── Dockerfile           # Multi-stage Android/Linux build system
├── go.mod               # Go module dependencies
├── server.go            # Cross-platform core: HTTP logic, Storage routing
├── main_desktop.go      # Build Tag (!android): Desktop browser autolauncher
├── main_android.go      # Build Tag (android): Mobile canvas and lifecycle manager
└── frontend/
    └── index.html       # Vanilla JS + Tailwind frontend interface
```

## 🏗️ Build Instructions

### 1. Generating the Source Files

Run the setup script inside an empty directory to write all files to disk:

```bash
python3 setup_project.py
```

### 2. Desktop Development & Testing

To run the server locally on your development machine (Linux/macOS/Windows):

```bash
# This will launch the Go backend and automatically open your default browser.
go run server.go main_desktop.go
```

### 3. Dockerized Multi-Architecture Build (Android + Linux)

We leverage a highly optimized, multi-stage Docker environment to compile the Android APK without installing Android Studio on the host.

```bash
# Build the Docker image (caches toolchains in Stage 1, packages in Stage 2)
docker build -t goomn-builder .

# Extract the compiled Desktop Binary and Android APK to your host
docker create --name goomn-extract goomn-builder
docker cp goomn-extract:/app/bin/ ./output-binaries/
docker rm goomn-extract

# View your Android APK
ls -lh ./output-binaries/goomn.apk
```
